creator: roam@ringlet.net
text:
Forwarded "for the benefit of Mr. GNATS".

G'luck,
Peter

-- 
If this sentence didn't exist, somebody would have invented it.

----- Forwarded message from Rahul Siddharthan <rsidd@physics.iisc.ernet.in> -----

Date: Fri, 14 Sep 2001 00:46:48 +0200
From: Rahul Siddharthan <rsidd@physics.iisc.ernet.in>
To: roam@FreeBSD.org
Cc: freebsd-ports@FreeBSD.org
Subject: Re: ports/30553: kdelibs2 (esp arts) should be built without threads
User-Agent: Mutt/1.2.5i
In-Reply-To: <200109131627.f8DGRvX16923@freefall.freebsd.org>; from roam@FreeBSD.org on Thu, Sep 13, 2001 at 09:27:57AM -0700
X-Operating-System: FreeBSD 4.4-PRERELEASE i386

roam@FreeBSD.org said on Sep 13, 2001 at 09:27:57:
> Synopsis: kdelibs2 (esp arts) should be built without threads
> 
> Can you try building kdelibs2 again, this time either without
> the pth port installed, or with the updated pth port 

Yes indeed, it works with the updated pth port.  My earlier pth was
about a week old.  

On that subject, how about an xmms-arts port?  I saw an archived
posting by someone who had tried but failed, a while ago, because
of basically the same problem I was having (arts_init couldn't open
libartscbackend.la).  Presumably it should work now.

> the pth port.  I think that kdelibs2 should work fine with FreeBSD's
> native thread libraries.

Sorry for my ignorance -- I did look in /usr/lib but couldn't find
anything which looked like threads.

In that case, why doesn't kdelibs2 just use FreeBSD's native threads,
instead of bothering with the pth port?

Thanks -- R


----- End forwarded message -----
